area_anchor: Reception inbox should suppress obvious non-guest promotional emails before they surface to staff
current_truth: Gmail organize and Reception sync each classify inbound email separately using similar heuristics, but Reception still rescans raw Gmail threads and can surface low-confidence non-guest promotional mail in the inbox. A recent example was the Italian promotional subject 'Il caldo arriva, gli infestanti anche. Proteggiti ora', which is clearly not guest mail.
next_scope_now: Implement a bounded classifier hardening pass so obvious vendor/newsletter/promotional subjects are treated as non-customer mail in both Gmail organize and Reception admission, with regression tests for the concrete Italian example.
adjacent_later: Unify Gmail organize and Reception admission onto one shared classifier module to remove rule drift.
Consider a quarantine lane for ambiguous unknown mail instead of surfacing review-later items in the main inbox.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: non-guest and OTA mail should not surface in the Reception inbox
operator-stated: the subject 'Il caldo arriva, gli infestanti anche. Proteggiti ora' is clearly not from a hostel guest
why: Staff time in the Reception inbox should be reserved for real guest and operational threads. When obvious promotional/vendor emails leak into the inbox, they dilute trust in the queue and create avoidable manual triage.

intended_outcome.statement: Reception suppresses obvious non-guest promotional/vendor emails consistently across Gmail organize and Reception admission, so staff no longer see them in the main inbox.
